coverage_limits: The supplied materials contain no protocol pseudocode or transition relation beyond the stated invariant and infinite schedule.
The assessment establishes failure of starvation-freedom on the supplied schedule; it does not assess deadlock-freedom, bounded waiting, or liveness under additional fairness assumptions.

rigor.trigger: The claim infers complete concurrent-protocol correctness from a safety invariant despite a supplied starvation trace.
rigor.tier_reason: A formal counterexample over the supplied synthetic schedule decisively assesses the claim without empirical or external evidence.

decision_frame.question: Does proof of mutual exclusion alone establish that the locking protocol is fully correct?
decision_frame.system_boundary: The locking protocol, processes A and B, and the supplied infinite execution schedule.
alternatives: Accept c1: mutual-exclusion safety is sufficient for full correctness.
Reject c1: full correctness also requires an independently established progress property.
hard_constraints: B remains enabled but never enters its critical section.
Only the supplied scenario and artifacts may be used.
authorized_objectives: Assess claim c1 using formal concurrency and specification theory.
decision_frame.priority_rule: A single admissible counterexample trace refutes a universal correctness claim.
assumptions: Fully correct locking includes safety and liveness obligations.
The supplied infinite schedule is admissible unless an explicit fairness premise excludes it.
decision_frame.uncertainty_posture: Do not infer unstated scheduler fairness or protocol behavior.

coverage.reason: No relation, dependency, key, or schema decomposition is involved.
The claim concerns a concurrent execution in which one enabled process never makes progress.
No replicas, partitions, quorums, or distributed consistency guarantees are present.
No workload complexity, recurrence, index, or asymptotic optimization claim is made.
Full correctness requires the implementation traces to satisfy both the invariant and the progress specification.
No entropy, encoding, hashing, or cardinality-estimation question is present.
No architectural duplication, coupling, materialized view, or failure-domain decision is present.
The supplied theory battery defines no section corresponding to family P8.
The supplied theory battery defines no section corresponding to family P9.

derivations.construct: Safety versus liveness over an infinite schedule; starvation-freedom and weak fairness

model.schedule: σ=(A acquires; A enters; A exits; A reacquires; …), while B remains enabled and never enters

preconditions: The artifact states that mutual exclusion holds.
The artifact supplies an infinite schedule on which A repeatedly reacquires and B remains enabled but never enters.
steps: Every state on σ may satisfy ¬(CS_A ∧ CS_B), so σ satisfies the mutual-exclusion safety invariant.
B is enabled/requesting on σ, but there is no later state in which B enters its critical section.
Therefore σ falsifies G(request_B → F CS_B), making it a counterexample to starvation-freedom.
Safety constrains forbidden finite prefixes; it does not entail that a desired event eventually occurs on every infinite execution.

If weak fairness were assumed, a continuously enabled B action would eventually execute and σ would be excluded, but no such premise is supplied; moreover, that liveness conclusion would follow from safety plus fairness, not from the safety invariant alone.
derivations.result: The protocol is mutual-exclusion-safe on the supplied facts but is not starvation-free on the supplied admissible schedule; safety alone cannot establish full correctness.
derivations.residual_mismatch: Protocol pseudocode is absent, so no stronger progress property or fairness-sensitive implementation proof can be derived.

derivations.construct: Total correctness and trace refinement: invariant preservation plus progress

primary_theory: current-v1-SKILL.md: lens 5 and single-option reversal rule

model.implementation_trace_set: Contains the supplied starving trace σ.
model.full_specification: All traces preserve mutual exclusion and every requesting enabled process eventually enters.

preconditions: Full locking correctness includes both mutual exclusion and progress.
Universal trace satisfaction is required to establish the full specification.

fact_mapping.violated_progress_obligation: B remains enabled forever and never enters on σ.

steps: Invariant preservation establishes only the safety component of the specification, analogous to partial correctness.
For trace refinement, every implementation trace must satisfy every required specification property.
The supplied σ belongs to the stated protocol behavior, satisfies the invariant, and violates the progress property.
Thus the implementation trace set is not a subset of the full-specification trace set.
One counterexample trace is sufficient to refute the universal declaration of full correctness.
derivations.result: c1 is refuted: the safety proof establishes mutual exclusion, not full protocol correctness.
derivations.residual_mismatch: A revised conditional claim could be evaluated only after stating a fairness assumption and proving the protocol's liveness under it.

synthesis.selected_option: Reject c1 and classify the protocol as mutual-exclusion-safe but not fully correct.
basis: The safety invariant holds on the supplied schedule.
The same schedule is a formal counterexample to starvation-freedom.
Safety does not logically imply liveness.
conditions: The verdict treats the supplied infinite schedule as admissible because no fairness premise excludes it.
concessions: The mutual-exclusion result remains established; only the inference to full correctness is rejected.
recovery_moves: State the required progress property explicitly.
Declare any scheduler fairness premise explicitly.
Prove starvation-freedom or bounded waiting under that premise, independently of the mutual-exclusion invariant.
